title: subworkflow - azure wait for automation runbook job
description:
Subworkflow to wait for an Azure Automation runbook job to finish

This subworkflow is used in subworkflows that execute Azure Automation runbooks
to wait for the job to finish and check the status of the execution.
If the job was not successful the subworkflow will fail so that
parent workflows can respond with rerun on error.

Parameters:

azuresubscriptionid: (Required) Azure Subscription ID
automationresourcegroup: (Required) Resource group of the Automation Account
automationaccountname: (Required) Azure Automation Account name
jobId: (Required) Job ID returned by the start runbook job action
dynatraceazureconnection: (Required) Dynatrace Azure OIDC connection name
guide:
## ⏳ Subworkflow to Wait for Azure Automation Runbook Job to Finish

🧠 This subworkflow is used by other subworkflows that **execute Azure Automation runbooks** to **wait for the job to complete** and **validate the execution status**.

✅ If the runbook job completes successfully, the subworkflow finishes.
❌ If the job fails or enters an error state, the subworkflow fails — allowing **parent workflows to respond with rerun-on-error or alternative handling logic**.

---

### ⚙️ Parameters

- 🔵 **azuresubscriptionid** (Required): Azure Subscription ID where the Automation Account resides.
- 📁 **automationresourcegroup** (Required): Resource group name of the Azure Automation Account.
- 🤖 **automationaccountname** (Required): Azure Automation Account that executed the runbook.
- 🧾 **jobId** (Required): The job ID returned by the start runbook job action.
- 🔑 **dynatraceazureconnection** (Required): Dynatrace Azure OIDC connection name.

---

### 🧠 Workflow Logic

- ⏳ Continuously checks the status of the specified Azure Automation runbook job
- ✅ If the job reaches `Completed` state, the subworkflow finishes successfully
- 🔁 If still `Running`, `Activating`, or `New`, it throws and retries (retry × 99, delay 60s)
- ❌ If the job ends in `Failed`, `Stopped`, `Suspended`, or `Blocked`, the subworkflow fails

---

### 🧩 Usage Context

- ✅ Designed as a **helper / control-flow subworkflow**
- ✅ Used by all Azure remediation subworkflows that trigger Automation runbooks
- ✅ Ensures downstream workflow steps only execute after runbook completion
- ✅ Commonly paired with VM remediation, database failover, and security response workflows

---

### 🔗 Dependencies

- 🔌 Requires a valid **Dynatrace Azure OIDC connection** configured in your environment
- 🤖 Requires the **`dynatrace.azure.connector:azure-automation-get-job`** action to be available

get-status.name: get-status
get-status.description: Get the current status of the Azure Automation runbook job.

check-status.name: check-status
check-status.description: Check Azure Automation runbook job status and retry if still running.
